prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG gaslamp-industrial atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Blades in the Dark / Doskvol inspired industrial-gothic world map, continent plus archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view of one dominant industrial-gothic continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago in the black Void Sea.
Subject: one main continent with complex coastlines, soot-black gulfs, bays, capes, narrow straits, broken industrial coasts, canal deltas, inland black seas, and many irregular island chains offshore. Islands must be unevenly distributed with varied sizes and shapes: smuggler islands, tiny haunted islets, leviathan-hunter ports, refinery islands, reef rings, lagoons, narrow channels, remote atolls, prison islands, ghost field ruins, and storm-lashed naval stations. Include Doskvol-like canal districts, lightning rail lines, leaky industrial waterways, walled city sectors, gang territory sigils, deathlands beyond walls, electroplasmic towers, fog marshes, coal hills, tundra, forests, mountains, wetlands, and ash plains.
Style/medium: Doskvol city district map expanded to continent scale, gothic industrial zones, criminal faction territories marked with non-text sigils, gaslamp fantasy atlas, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, dominant continent offset to one side and archipelago sweeping diagonally around it, no simple grid spacing, full map visible.
Lighting/mood: industrial gothic, perpetual supernatural darkness, smog and fog, flickering gas lamps, electroplasmic blue lightning, dangerous smuggling routes, hungry ghosts and criminal factions.
Color palette: charcoal black, tarnished brass, cold blue electroplasm, sickly green fog, soot grey, muted brick red, oil-black ocean.
Materials/textures: blackened parchment, etched coastlines, gaslight glow, soot stains, canal-line geometry, ghostly blue technical overlays.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Islands should feel natural and irregular, not evenly spaced.
